violations.description: File is 1,455 LOC (4.85x the 300 LOC DoD limit) with 81 [Fact]/[Theory] methods spanning Tenant lifecycle, user membership, RBAC, role escalation, and state replay. The single-class structure makes navigation, code review, and merge-conflict resolution painful.
violations.suggestion: Split into TenantLifecycleTests, TenantUserMembershipTests, TenantRbacTests, and TenantStateReplayTests by domain area; each file should stay under 300 LOC.
violations.code_snippet: 1,455 LOC, 81 [Fact]/[Theory] — largest test file in the suite

violations.description: File is 1,169 LOC (3.9x the 300 LOC limit) with 54 [Fact]/[Theory] methods covering compliance assertions, conformance suite expectations, and aggregate behavior validation.
violations.suggestion: Decompose by conformance dimension (lifecycle, RBAC, persistence, multi-tenancy) into separate files.
violations.code_snippet: 1,169 LOC, 54 test methods, includes a private AssertEventsEqual helper at line 1143

violations.description: File is 1,109 LOC (3.7x the limit) with 50 [Fact]/[Theory] methods. Has grown by 79 LOC since the 2026-04-18 review (1,030 -> 1,109), confirming the file is on a runaway trajectory.
violations.suggestion: Split into ProcessAsync_PayloadDispatch, ProcessAsync_StateRehydration, ProcessAsync_RecordAggregates, and ProcessAsync_TerminationSemantics test classes.
violations.code_snippet: Grew from 1,030 to 1,109 LOC since 2026-04-18 — DoD violation worsening, not stabilising

violations.description: File is 934 LOC (3.1x the limit) with 22 test methods, four of which exceed the 80 LOC method-size threshold (89, 83, 104, 94 LOC) — combined file-size + method-size DoD breach.
violations.suggestion: Extract TrackIdentityAsync_* tests into ProjectionCheckpointTrackerIdentityTrackingTests; keep checkpoint persistence tests in the original file.
violations.code_snippet: Methods >80 LOC: TrackIdentityAsync_RetryAfterPriorFailure (89), TrackIdentityAsync_FullIdentityPage_AppendsToNextPageAndUpdatesIndex (94), TrackIdentityAsync_AggregateIdentityNormalization (83), TrackIdentityAsync_MixedAndLowerCaseInputs (104)

violations.description: File is 813 LOC (2.7x the limit). Contains the StateManager reflection duplication anti-pattern.
violations.suggestion: Split by trace-chain stage (origin capture, propagation, consumer) and adopt the existing AggregateActorTestHelper.AttachStateManager pattern.
violations.code_snippet: 813 LOC + reflection duplication at line 81

violations.description: File is 808 LOC (2.7x the limit) with two distinct StateManager reflection sites (line 43, line 63) that should call into the existing AggregateActorTestHelper.
violations.suggestion: Split by recovery phase (drain start, retry, completion); centralise StateManager reflection.
violations.code_snippet: 808 LOC + 2 reflection sites at lines 43, 63

violations.description: File is 793 LOC (2.6x limit). Contains the only test method exceeding 120 LOC in the suite (QueryCacheTopology_WhenProjectionChanges_InvalidatesOldETagAndCachesNewETag, 121 LOC) AND three private Assert* helper methods that hide ShouldBeTrue/ShouldThrow assertions inside named helpers.
violations.suggestion: Split into per-scenario classes; rename Assert* helpers to neutral names (e.g. WarmCacheNotModifiedScenario) that signal they perform multi-step orchestration, not just an assertion.
violations.code_snippet: Hidden assertions at lines 349 (AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync), 391 (AssertExpectedCountETagContract), 496 (AssertPersistedEventEvidence), 508 (AssertStrongSelfRoutingETag)

violations.description: File is 771 LOC (2.6x limit) with 32 [Fact]/[Theory] methods; representative of unsplit controller test files across the suite.
violations.suggestion: Split by HTTP verb / endpoint: QueriesController_GetTests, QueriesController_PostTests, QueriesController_AuthorizationTests.
violations.code_snippet: 771 LOC, 32 test methods

violations.description: File is 753 LOC (2.5x limit). Has a `// Story 11-3 Task 6:` story-number comment at line 29 that will rot relative to commit history per CLAUDE.md guidance.
violations.suggestion: Split by orchestration concern; remove story-number annotations.
violations.code_snippet: 753 LOC + story-number rot at line 29

violations.description: File is 709 LOC (2.4x limit). Final entry in the >700 LOC DoD-breaching cohort.
violations.suggestion: Split by cache scenario (hit, miss, eviction, invalidation).
violations.code_snippet: 709 LOC

violations.description: An additional 80 test files exceed the 300 LOC DoD limit beyond the top 10 itemised above — total 90 violating files across the suite (632 test files total, ~14% violate DoD). The pattern is systemic, not isolated. Examples include AuthorizationBehaviorTests (676), DeadLettersPageTests (666), EventStoreSignalRClientTests (648), AccessControlPolicyTests (640), DeadLetterTraceChainTests (631), AssemblyScannerTests (621), TenantsPageTests (608), EventPublisherTests (601), AggregateActorDomainResultTests (583), DaprTestContainerFixture (562), PersistThenPublishResilienceTests (560), NamingConventionEngineTests (559).
violations.suggestion: Treat the 300 LOC limit as a hard pre-merge check, not advisory. Add a CI guard that fails when any file under tests/ or Hexalith.Tenants/tests/ exceeds 300 LOC.
violations.code_snippet: 90 of 632 test files (~14%) violate the 300 LOC DoD; cohort spans 309-1455 LOC

violations.description: The pattern `typeof(Actor).GetProperty("StateManager", BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.Instance)` followed by `prop?.SetValue(actor, stateManager)` is duplicated across 24 test files (32 occurrences) despite an existing AggregateActorTestHelper that already encapsulates the pattern at line 75. The helper exists; tests do not call it.
violations.suggestion: Extract a public static `ActorTestHarness.AttachStateManager<TActor>(TActor actor, IActorStateManager mock)` and migrate all 32 sites. Estimated ~96 LOC eliminated (3 LOC per site × 32 sites) and a single point of failure if Dapr renames the property.
violations.code_snippet: Files: Telemetry/EndToEndTraceTests.cs:52; Observability/DeadLetterTraceChainTests.cs:77,138,191; Observability/DeadLetterOriginTracingTests.cs:81; Observability/DeadLetterMessageCompletenessTests.cs:72; Security/TenantInjectionPreventionTests.cs:74,112,152; Events/AtLeastOnceDeliveryTests.cs:268; Security/SecurityAuditLoggingTests.cs:142; Events/PersistThenPublishResilienceTests.cs:66; Integration/ETagActorIntegrationTests.cs:328; Security/DataPathIsolationTests.cs:144,203,254; Actors/StateMachineIntegrationTests.cs:60; Actors/MultiTenantPublicationTests.cs:55,127,169,217; Actors/EventReplayProjectionActorTests.cs:37; Actors/EventPublicationIntegrationTests.cs:57; Actors/EventDrainRecoveryTests.cs:43,63; Actors/ETagActorTests.cs:35; Actors/DeadLetterRoutingTests.cs:66; Actors/BackpressureTests.cs:58; Actors/AggregateActorGetEventsTests.cs:49

violations.description: Five private reflection helpers (InvokeProjectionChanged at 587, InvokeOnReconnectedAsync at 595, InvokeOnClosedAsync at 606, plus the ReconnectConfigurator nested-type accessor at 620 and the private constructor invocation at 626) reach into private members of EventStoreSignalRClient. The repeated GetMethod(..., BindingFlags.NonPublic) pattern indicates the SUT exposes too little public API for testability.
violations.suggestion: Refactor EventStoreSignalRClient to expose protected/internal hooks (e.g. via [InternalsVisibleTo] or a TestHook seam) so tests do not need 5 reflection sites.
violations.code_snippet: Lines 587-635: 5 separate reflection accessors for OnProjectionChanged, OnReconnectedAsync, OnClosedAsync, ReconnectConfigurator nested type, and a private constructor

violations.description: Helpers named `Assert*` perform multi-step orchestration that includes assertions but also state mutations and request issuance. Naming a helper Assert* implies a single-purpose check; readers expecting that surface are misled. Same anti-pattern present in another 14 files (DaprTestContainerFixture, AccessControlPolicyTests, PayloadProtectionTests, SignalRRedisBackplaneRuntimeProofTests, ValidProjectionRoundTripE2ETests, OpenApiE2ETests, HttpStaleETagProofE2ETests, ResiliencyConfigurationTests, ProblemTypeUriComplianceTests, EventDrainRecoveryTests, PubSubTopicIsolationEnforcementTests, TenantsDaprTestFixture, AspireTopologyFixture, TenantConformanceTests).
violations.suggestion: Rename helpers to action-oriented verbs (e.g. AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync -> EnsureWarmRequestReturnsNotModifiedAndCaptureETag); keep pure assertions narrowly scoped.
violations.code_snippet: private async Task AssertWarmNotModifiedAsync(QueryIdentity query, string baselineETag, string firstCorrelationId, ...) — issues HTTP requests, captures ETags, and asserts; not a pure check

violations.description: TenantAggregateTests uses `snake_case_with_underscores_throughout` style (e.g. `CreateTenant_with_no_prior_state_produces_TenantCreated`) — 59 of 81 test methods follow the underscore-rich `Method_with_scenario_words_produces_outcome` style instead of the codebase-standard `Method_Scenario_Expected` Pascal-style scenario seen in EventStoreAggregateTests.cs (45/50 matching) and ProjectionCheckpointTrackerTests.cs (21/22 matching). Reviewer-team consistency suffers.
violations.suggestion: Choose one convention project-wide (Method_Scenario_Expected matches the .NET community norm) and refactor TenantAggregateTests to match. Document the convention in CLAUDE.md.
violations.code_snippet: Sample naming in TenantAggregateTests (snake-style): CreateTenant_with_no_prior_state_produces_TenantCreated, AddUserToTenant_when_user_already_member_produces_UserAlreadyInTenantRejection, ChangeUserRole_with_undefined_role_produces_RoleEscalationRejection. Sample in EventStoreAggregateTests (Pascal-scenario): ProcessAsync_MatchingHandleMethod_DispatchesCorrectly, ProcessAsync_NullState_PassesNullToHandle

violations.description: 12 test methods exceed the 80 LOC threshold across the suite. Worst offender: QueryCacheTopology_WhenProjectionChanges_InvalidatesOldETagAndCachesNewETag at 121 LOC. Other notable offenders: PostCommands_SignedHappyPath_ReachesCompletedWithEvent (112), RedisBackplane_TwoEventStoreInstances_DeliversProjectionChangedAcrossInstances (110), ProcessCommand_CrashAtEventsStored_Resume_DoesNotRePersistEvents (102), TrackIdentityAsync_MixedAndLowerCaseInputsForSameAggregate_ConvergeOnSamePersistedIdentity (104), TrackIdentityAsync_FullIdentityPage_AppendsToNextPageAndUpdatesIndex (94), TenantBootstrap_FirstSixtySeconds_NoFailureEvents (96), TrackIdentityAsync_RetryAfterPriorFailure_CompletesRegistrationOnSecondCall (89), TrackIdentityAsync_AggregateIdentityNormalization_FlowsCanonicalLowercaseToTrackerPersistence (83), StateMachineIntegrationTests:ProcessCommand_CrashAtEventsStored_Resume_PreservesResultPayload (86).
violations.suggestion: Extract Arrange-heavy logic into private builders or shared fixtures; aim to keep test bodies under 40 LOC.
violations.code_snippet: Single-Fact 121-LOC method; readability and review cost grows superlinearly with length

violations.description: 10 test classes exceed 30 [Fact]/[Theory] methods, hindering code review and IDE navigation: TenantAggregateTests (81), TenantConformanceTests (54), NamingConventionEngineTests (51), EventStoreAggregateTests (50), AssemblyScannerTests (44), AggregateIdentityTests (38), EventStoreSignalRClientTests (35), MessageTypeTests (35), SnapshotManagerTests (32), QueriesControllerTests (32).
violations.suggestion: Apply the same domain-area-split rule as for oversized files; aim for <20 [Fact]/[Theory] methods per class.
violations.code_snippet: 10 classes >30 test methods; top class has 81

violations.description: Story/Epic identifiers are embedded in 88 test files (164 occurrences total). Examples: NamingConventionEngineTests:447 `// Story 18-4: GetQueryTypeName tests (Task 8)`, OpenTelemetryRegistrationTests:10 `/// Story 6.1 Task 7: OpenTelemetry registration tests.`, DeadLetterOriginTracingTests:37 `/// Story 6.3: Dead-letter-to-origin tracing tests.`, ProjectionUpdateOrchestratorTests:29 `/// Story 11-3 Task 6: ProjectionUpdateOrchestrator tests.`. These references rot relative to commit history (story IDs change between sprints; tasks renumber).
violations.suggestion: Strip story/epic/task identifiers from XML doc-comments and inline comments. Keep semantic descriptions of WHY the test exists, not the planning artifact.
violations.code_snippet: 164 Story/Epic comment occurrences across 88 distinct test files

violations.description: Lines 411, 429, 444, 459 are 4 near-identical [Fact] tests that differ only in the JSON payload string — perfect [Theory] candidates with [InlineData]. Same pattern repeats around lines 332, 342, 356 (state-rehydration variants). These could collapse to ~2 Theory methods reducing ~80 LOC.
violations.suggestion: Convert ProcessAsync_JsonElementArray_With{UnknownEventType,NonObjectEntry,MissingEventTypeName,InvalidPayloadShape} into a single [Theory] with [InlineData] for each invalid JSON shape.

violations.description: DomainResultAssertionsTests.cs mixes 4 xUnit Assert.* calls with 8 Shouldly Should* calls within the same file. The Hexalith.EventStore.Client.Tests/Aggregates/EventStoreAggregateTests.cs is xUnit-only (98 Assert.*, 0 Shouldly) while CLAUDE.md prescribes Shouldly fluent assertions — broader inconsistency exists between projects (Sample.Tests, Testing.Tests use Shouldly; Client.Tests, Server.Tests largely use xUnit Assert).
violations.suggestion: Adopt CLAUDE.md's stated standard (Shouldly) project-wide; add an .editorconfig analyzer rule banning xUnit Assert.Equal/True/False/NotNull in tests.
violations.code_snippet: DomainResultAssertionsTests: 4 xUnit Assert vs 8 Shouldly Should* in one file. Cross-project: EventStoreAggregateTests is 100% xUnit (98 Assert.*); GreetingAggregateTests is 100% Shouldly.

violations.description: Test traits ([Trait("Tier", "...")] / [Trait("Category", "...")]) are present in 36 of 581 .NET test files in tests/ (6.2% coverage). Sub-project breakdown: Server.Tests has 5 of 169 files trait-tagged (3.0%); Admin.Server.Tests has 3 of 58 (5.2%); IntegrationTests has 27 of 68 (39.7%). Selective trait usage means Tier/Category-based test selection in CI is unreliable.
violations.suggestion: Either trait-tag every test class in projects that use traits OR drop traits entirely. CLAUDE.md tier model (1/2/3) implies traits should be universal in IntegrationTests at minimum.
violations.code_snippet: Server.Tests trait coverage 5/169; IntegrationTests trait coverage 27/68; same project, inconsistent practice

recommendations: Add a hard CI guard (e.g. a roslyn analyzer or a simple PowerShell wc -l pre-merge check) that fails the build when any file under tests/ exceeds 300 LOC. 90 files (~14% of the suite) are out of compliance and the trend is worsening (EventStoreAggregateTests grew 79 LOC since 2026-04-18). Prioritise splitting the 11 oversized files >700 LOC first; they account for ~9,300 LOC of the violation budget.

Build a public ActorTestHarness helper class (e.g. tests/Hexalith.EventStore.Testing/Harness/ActorTestHarness.cs) exposing AttachStateManager<TActor>, CreateActorWithDefaults<TActor>, and a TraceContextScope. Migrate all 32 StateManager-reflection sites and the 5 SignalR private-member sites. Estimated reduction: ~150 LOC across 25 files plus a single point of failure when Dapr SDK changes the property name.

Strip Story/Epic identifiers from 164 inline test comments across 88 files. Keep WHY the test exists, drop the WHO (sprint reference). Add a CLAUDE.md rule banning sprint identifiers in source files (they belong in commit messages and the issue tracker).
summary: The Hexalith.EventStore test suite is functionally rich but structurally over-grown: 90 of 632 test files (14%) violate the 300 LOC DoD, 11 of those exceed 700 LOC, and EventStoreAggregateTests has actively grown by 79 LOC since the prior review. A single high-impact reflection pattern (typeof(Actor).GetProperty("StateManager")) duplicates across 32 sites in 24 files despite an existing helper, indicating the maintainability discipline is reactive rather than enforced. Fix-the-process recommendations (CI line-count guard, mandatory ActorTestHarness usage, story-comment ban) outweigh per-file cleanup in expected ROI.
